channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Putco announces 10% fare increase from June amid rising fuel prices
item.description: Commuters in Gauteng are facing steeper travel costs as both Putco buses and minibus taxis prepare for fare increases. Putco has announced a 10% fare hike from 1 June due to rising diesel prices linke...

item.title: Recognising Common Signs of Drug Use in Loved Ones
item.description: Drug use has become increasingly common in the UK, affecting people across all ages and social backgrounds. According to the Crime Survey for England and Wales, 8.7 per cent of people aged 16 to 59 re...

item.title: Flagship Minerals raises A$2.75M to advance Isidora gold project in Chile
item.description: Flagship Minerals has successfully raised A$2.75 million through a strategic placement to accelerate development at its Isidora gold project in Chile. The funding will support key technical workstream...

item.title: Lawyer Criticizes Supreme Court's Use of Purcell Principle in Election Cases
item.description: During a recent episode of his 'Democracy Docket' podcast, attorney Marc Elias criticized the Supreme Court for its inconsistent application of the Purcell Principle in election law cases. Elias descr...

item.title: FTC Settles with Companies Misleading Businesses About Audio-Based Ad Targeting
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced that Cox Media Group, MindSift LLC, and 1010 Digital Works will collectively pay nearly $1 million to settle claims that they misled clients about a market...

item.title: President Ruto Highlights Major Development Projects Along Kenya Coast
item.description: President William Ruto has reiterated the government's commitment to transforming Kenya’s Coast region, which has historically lagged due to marginalisation and retrogressive politics. During a develo...

item.title: US Introduces Visa Restrictions on South Sudan Officials Over Human Rights Concerns
item.description: The United States has taken action to restrict visas for certain members of South Sudan's government and military. This decision comes as Washington grows frustrated with the lack of progress in peace...

item.title: Gun Control Groups Seek Transparency on Firearms Used in Tumbler Ridge Mass Shooting
item.description: Gun control advocacy organisations, including PolySeSouvient and Danforth Families for Safe Communities, are pressing the RCMP to provide more detailed information about the firearms involved in the t...

item.title: Xavier Becerra Discusses AI, Healthcare, and Election Plans in CNN Interview
item.description: In a recent interview with CNN, gubernatorial candidate and former U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services, Xavier Becerra, shared his perspectives on several pressing issues. Becerra discussed th...

item.title: Shakira to feature Uganda’s Ghetto Kids in 2026 World Cup halftime show
item.description: Shakira, the famous Colombian singer, has announced that Uganda’s popular dance group, Ghetto Kids, will join her for the halftime performance at the 2026 World Cup final. She revealed the news via In...

item.title: WA Premier Sparks Debate Over Kimberley Gas and Energy Security
item.description: Western Australian Premier Roger Cook has stirred debate after suggesting that fracking in the Kimberley could become necessary if Woodside’s Browse gas project does not proceed. He cited forecasts of...

item.title: Stars Perform at Cannes' amfAR Gala in Support of AIDS Research
item.description: The annual amfAR Gala, part of the Cannes Film Festival, took place on the French Riviera in Antibes, offering a glamorous evening of charity, performances, and auctions to support AIDS research. Atte...

item.title: US Temporarily Suspends Arms Sales to Taiwan Amid Rising Tensions in Iran
item.description: A top official from the United States Navy has confirmed that arms sales to Taiwan have been temporarily put on hold due to the ongoing conflict involving Iran. This pause is reportedly a precautionar...

item.title: How Nigeria’s PAYE Tax Structure Affects State Development And Stakeholders
item.description: This article yan about how Nigeria dey struggle with the current PAYE tax system and how e dey affect development across different states. Lagos State don show say e be like small country because of t...

item.title: Early Life and Musical Journey of Annie Lennox
item.description: Annie Lennox, born on December 25, 1954, in Torry, Aberdeen, Scotland, is renowned as a Scottish singer-songwriter, activist, and philanthropist. She began exploring her artistic abilities at Aberdeen...

item.title: Overview of the Upcoming Film 'Tayo Sa Wakas'
item.description: ABS-CBN, one of the Philippines’ leading media and entertainment companies, has shared details about its upcoming film 'Tayo Sa Wakas'. The movie is a Star Cinema production and features popular young...

item.title: Félicien Kabuga, Key Figure in 1994 Rwanda Genocide, Dies in The Hague Before Trial
item.description: Félicien Kabuga, who was one of the last major fugitives wanted for his role in the 1994 Rwanda genocide, has passed away while in detention in The Hague, Netherlands. Kabuga, who was in his late eigh...

item.title: A Glimpse into Teenage Sharon Tate’s Life in the 1950s
item.description: Sharon Tate, born in 1943, was an American actress and model whose early life in the 1950s was marked by frequent moves due to her father's military career. Living as an 'army brat,' she attended scho...

item.title: Impact of Trump Administration Science Policies on U.S. Researchers and Academic Research
item.description: Since the beginning of the second Trump administration in January 2025, U.S. academic researchers have faced significant challenges due to new federal policies affecting funding, diversity, equity, in...

item.title: Sudan Starts 3.4 Billion Pound Development Projects to Boost Economy
item.description: The Sudanese government has officially begun work on development projects worth 3.4 billion Sudanese pounds. This is a major move aimed at improving the country's infrastructure and supporting economi...
